text: Artificial intelligence (AI) is growing within the media and journalists are starting to embrace AI as an important tool in the newsroom. Despite AI contributing significantly to productivity, ethics is standing in the way of its full implementation. This research explores the relations between technology and media ethics based on the principles of Social Responsibility Theory and the moral philosophy school of thought. Using qualitative research method, a thorough thematic analysis was done on interviews of 14 journalists working at a state-owned channel in Jordan named Al Mamlaka TV. The findings of the study concluded that three ethical challenges stand in the way of the implementation of AI in media: the first is related to data bias, the second is the breach of privacy and the third is the absence of international laws and policies to regulate the use of technologies. The study found that journalists in Al Mamlaka TV implement critical adoption in the use of emerging technologies in order to preserve the principles of social responsibility and in this way preserve journalistic professionalism in the new media era.
headline: Fully Human Written
